pi_items.source: Task briefing explicitly assigns the role of synoptic meteorologist preparing a WMO RA VI Regional Training workshop diagnostic.
Task context is preparing teaching materials for a WMO workshop on hemispheric circulation patterns.

Both files verified present in live environment. air.mon.ltm.nc is 657356 bytes, slp.mon.ltm.nc is 552234 bytes. Both are real NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis long-term mean datasets downloaded from NOAA PSL (https://downloads.psl.noaa.gov/). Panoply process confirmed launched with both files as arguments.
Directory created by setup_task.sh, verified as owned by ga:ga with 755 permissions. Write permissions confirmed by test file creation/deletion in live environment.

All three output paths are referenced in setup_task.sh (cleanup), export_result.sh (existence/size/mtime checks), and the briefing document. export_result.sh was tested live and correctly checks all three files.
Briefing document specifies January. In the NCEP data, January is time index 0 (the first of 12 monthly long-term means). Live netCDF4 extraction confirmed January data at index 0.

Direct netCDF4 extraction from slp.mon.ltm.nc: January SLP at (65.0N, 340.0E) = 998.59 millibars (= 998.59 hPa). This is an exact grid point (2.5-degree grid). Value 998.59 falls within [995, 1010]. The Icelandic Low is a well-documented semi-permanent low-pressure center in the NCEP/NCAR climatology.

Direct netCDF4 extraction from slp.mon.ltm.nc: January SLP at (50.0N, 90.0E) = 1041.99 millibars (= 1041.99 hPa). This is an exact grid point. Value 1041.99 falls within [1025, 1045]. This is also the global maximum January SLP in the dataset (global max = 1041.99), confirming the Siberian High is the dominant pressure center.
Direct netCDF4 extraction from slp.mon.ltm.nc: January SLP at (50.0N, 180.0E) = 997.59 millibars (= 997.59 hPa). This is an exact grid point. Value 997.59 falls within [995, 1012].
Calculated from verified values: Siberian High (1041.99) minus Icelandic Low (998.59) = 43.40 hPa. Value 43.40 falls within [20, 50].

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis long-term mean climatology data from NOAA PSL. A critical data quirk: the SLP variable in slp.mon.ltm.nc is stored in millibars (equivalent to hPa), NOT in Pascals. The briefing contains a conditional note about unit conversion; since the data is already in millibars, no division by 100 is needed. If the agent incorrectly divides by 100, it will report nonsensical values (~9.99 hPa instead of ~998.59 hPa). The air temperature variable in air.mon.ltm.nc is in degrees Celsius (not Kelvin). Verified January SLP ground truth values at exact 2.5-degree grid points: Icelandic Low at (65.0N, 340.0E) = 998.59 hPa, Siberian High at (50.0N, 90.0E) = 1041.99 hPa, Aleutian Low at (50.0N, 180.0E) = 997.59 hPa. The pressure contrast (Siberian minus Icelandic) = 43.40 hPa. The Siberian High value (1041.99) is the global January SLP maximum, making Siberia the correct DOMINANT_HIGH_REGION. The dataset has 12 monthly time steps; January is time index 0. The grid is 73 latitudes by 144 longitudes (2.5-degree spacing). All three task coordinate locations fall on exact grid points, so no interpolation ambiguity exists. The agent must create three deliverables: (1) a Combine Plot overlay of air temperature color fill with SLP contour lines in North Polar Stereographic projection centered at 90N, (2) a 1D Line Plot of zonally averaged January SLP along the latitude axis, and (3) a structured text report with six key-value fields. Panoply's Stereographic projection is available in the Map Projection dropdown and the Center on Lat field accepts 90 for North Polar centering.
